archetype_reasoning: This trader operates across 74 distinct geo markets with solid win rates in Russia/UKR (66.3%), Iran/ME (72%), Europe (67.2%), and Trump/US (65.8%), suggesting real multi-domain forecasting ability rather than niche repetition. Directionality of 0.918 indicates strong directional conviction rather than LP behavior. However, open positions show a notable mix of near-certainty yield-harvesting trades (YES at 0.997, NO at 0.999) alongside genuinely contested entries, creating a hybrid profile that slightly dilutes the pure forecaster classification.
domain_blindspots: China_Asia: only 7 trades with 14.3% accuracy — near-random performance, possible overconfidence in unfamiliar geopolitics
Trump_US: near-certainty bets on government shutdown at 0.97-0.982 that lost, indicating yield-harvesting errors bleeding into this domain
domain_summary: Strongest genuine edge in Iran/ME (72% on 75 trades) and Europe (67.2% on 61 trades), both with meaningful sample sizes. Russia/UKR and Trump/US are solid but noisy, with some near-certainty losses suggesting the trader occasionally drifts into harvesting behavior and gets caught on tail events.
signal_weight_reasoning: The trader shows real forecasting skill across multiple domains with respectable accuracy and a positive realized PnL of ~$5,400. However, the high directionality (0.918) combined with near-certainty open positions (multiple at 0.997-0.999) suggests contamination from yield-harvesting behavior, particularly in Trump/US. The geo_elo_active of 2093 falls below the LEGENDARY threshold, and China_Asia results are nearly worthless. PARTIAL weight is appropriate — trust directional calls in Iran/ME and Europe at contested prices, discount near-certainty trades and China/Asia signals entirely.
behavioural_flags: Repeated entries in the same market (Israel x Hamas ceasefire: 7 recent trades), suggesting averaging-in or doubling-down behavior
Near-certainty yield harvesting in open positions: multiple NO positions at 0.987-0.999 on Iran-related military events
Entry at contested prices in some markets (0.26, 0.48, 0.49 on ceasefire) alongside high-conviction late entries (0.77) — inconsistent entry discipline
Government shutdown trades at 0.97 and 0.982 resulted in losses — chasing near-certainty and getting burned by tail events
NYC Mamdani NO trades at 0.051-0.055 are borderline yield-harvesting wins, not genuine forecasting
TIER_UPDATED_2026-06-15: ELITE → NEAR_LEGENDARY (geo_elo_active=2081.61, ELO recalculated post June 10 profiling). No archetype change.
risk_patterns: Sunk-cost or averaging behavior: multiple ceasefire positions across wide price range suggests reluctance to cut losing thesis

Concentration risk in Iran/ME open positions: multiple large positions on related Iran military inaction themes (Israel invade Iran, Iran strike Israel, US invade Iran) — correlated tail risk if regional escalation occurs
Near-certainty position sizing: $3,931 shares at 0.997 and $3,261 at 0.999 represent large nominal exposure for minimal expected return — asymmetric downside if black swan event occurs
Tucker Carlson 2028 NO at 0.946 is a very long-dated near-certainty trade with capital tied up for years

notable_calls.market: Israel x Hamas ceasefire by Oct 31
notable_calls.significance: Low-price contested entry that paid off — genuine forecasting judgment at underpriced odds
Near-certainty trade that lost — illustrates yield-harvesting risk when tail events materialize
Second position doubling down on near-certainty shutdown bet at even higher price — compounded yield-harvesting error
Extremely low-price NO on daily Iran strike — borderline yield-harvesting but reflects Iran/ME domain knowledge
Genuine contested long on Iran nuclear deal — meaningful directional call in core domain at fair odds

open_positions_summary: Portfolio implies two distinct theses: (1) Iran/ME de-escalation — multiple large NO positions on Israeli/US military action against Iran and Iranian regime collapse, with one contrarian YES on a nuclear deal at 0.22, suggesting this trader believes diplomacy is underpriced and military action overpriced; (2) Near-certainty yield collection — large positions at 0.987-0.999 on obvious outcomes (Trump lowers China tariffs, Trump attends NATO summit, US doesn't invade Iran) that provide minimal return for significant tail risk exposure. The US-Venezuela military engagement YES at 0.57 is the most genuinely contested open call.
watch_items: Monitor Iran/ME escalation events — trader has large correlated short-volatility exposure across multiple related markets; any escalation could trigger simultaneous losses
Track whether ceasefire-averaging behavior continues — repeated entries in the same market at declining prices is a potential sunk-cost flag
Watch US-Venezuela position at 0.57 as a signal of this trader's LatAm forecasting ability (currently only 1 resolved LatAm trade)
Monitor China_Asia entries if any appear — current 14.3% accuracy there should trigger immediate discount of any signals
Track if near-certainty yield positions resolve correctly or if tail-event losses recur as seen with government shutdown trades
summary: A capable multi-domain forecaster with real edge in Iran/ME and Europe, demonstrated by 72% and 67.2% accuracy on substantial trade samples. The $5,400 positive PnL and 74-market breadth confirm genuine forecasting ability rather than lucky specialization. However, the trader has a persistent yield-harvesting tendency — particularly visible in near-certainty open positions and the government shutdown losses — which contaminates the signal and introduces correlated tail risk. Best used as a PARTIAL signal source: weight directional calls in Iran/ME and Europe at contested prices (0.10-0.80), disregard China/Asia entirely, and flag near-certainty positions as unreliable indicators of forecasting skill.
